Name: Young Performers Theatre
Tagline: trike leaves San Francisco students unsupervised, a mismatched group of kids from different schools sets off on an unexpected adventure in the Presidio.
About: Through Theatre in Education and outreach programs, repertory productions, classes and summer camps, YPT helps children build core social-emotional learning skills and sets the stage for success in many fields of endeavor outside of performance. Bringing enrichment programming to schools, child-care facilities and community centers in the Bay Area, with an emphasis on providing a range of options — from classes and workshops to fully staged productions — tailored to the youth-development needs and cultural identities of the institutions served.
Philosophy: Matilda was passionate about serving youth who lacked access to theatre arts-based enrichment opportunities. She approached theatre not just as a performance medium but as a means of improving outcomes in early childhood development through creative problem solving, teamwork, focus, empathy and self-confidence.
Outcomes: Young Performers Theatre alumni have gone on to attend prestigious universities and have successful careers in the arts. Many students have received awards for excellence in theatre and performance.
Admissions: To apply to Young Performers Theatre, prospective students must complete an online application and attend an audition. Enrollment is open year-round, but spaces are limited and fill quickly.
Quote: Empowering young hearts through theater to build skills, inspire creativity, and illuminate paths to future success.

Performance.Coaching Credentials: Young Performers Theatre employs instructors with degrees in theatre arts and education, many holding over a decade of teaching experience. Staff members are certified in child development and specialize in drama-based learning techniques.
Performance.Student Wellbeing: Young Performers Theatre offers a dedicated student advisory program, providing personalized guidance and support. Regular workshops focus on building resilience, emotional intelligence, and peer collaboration, fostering a nurturing community environment.
Performance.Academic Integration: The curriculum includes courses in improvisation, script analysis, and stagecraft, emphasizing experiential learning. Students engage in project-based activities that integrate core academic subjects with theatre arts, enhancing critical thinking skills.
Performance.Competitive Pathway: Graduates have been accepted into top-tier institutions like Juilliard and NYU Tisch School of the Arts. Alumni frequently win regional theatre competitions, showcasing their exceptional talent and training.
Performance.Facilities & Resources: Young Performers Theatre boasts a state-of-the-art black box theater and multiple rehearsal studios. The facility includes costume and prop workshops, providing comprehensive resources for diverse theatrical productions.
Performance.Ongoing Accountability: Student progress is monitored through bi-annual performance reviews and personalized feedback sessions. Parents receive detailed progress reports and are invited to regular meetings to discuss student development.
